summary: @wagni_bot/opensea-sdk postinstall exfiltrates host and filesystem data
details: Package ships a postinstall.js that runs automatically on npm install. The script imports fs, os, and http, and collects host identity via os.hostname(), os.userInfo(), and os.homedir(), then probes filesystem paths (multiple fs.existsSync calls at lines 31, 83, 97) beneath the user's home directory. The combination of home-directory reconnaissance with an http client at install-time, in a package presenting itself as an OpenSea SDK, matches an installer-side credential/wallet reconnaissance and exfiltration pattern rather than any legitimate SDK install step. Name also impersonates the OpenSea brand namespace, consistent with a lure package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
